title: Alfvén Velocity

expressions.name: Alfvén velocity
expressions.expression: v_A = B / sqrt(4 * pi * mu * m_p * n); v_A in km/s

body: The Alfvén velocity is the characteristic speed of magnetohydrodynamic waves propagating along magnetic field lines. The Alfvén crossing time gives the timescale for magnetic communication across a region of size L.

title: Bondi Accretion

expressions.name: Bondi radius
expressions.expression: r_B = G * M / c_s^2; r_B in cm

expressions.name: Bondi accretion rate
expressions.expression: Mdot = 4 * pi * G^2 * M^2 * rho_inf / c_s^3; Mdot in M_sun/yr

expressions.name: Bondi-Hoyle accretion rate
expressions.expression: Mdot = 4 * pi * G^2 * M^2 * rho_inf / (c_s^2 + v_inf^2)^(3/2); Mdot in M_sun/yr

body: Bondi accretion describes the spherical accretion of gas onto a compact object. The Bondi-Hoyle interpolation extends this to account for the relative motion between the object and the gas.

title: Dynamical Friction

expressions.name: Deceleration (subsonic, v_M << sigma)
expressions.expression: dv = 4 * sqrt(2*pi) / 3 * G^2 * M * m_p * n * sigma / sigma^3 * log(Lambda); dv in cm/s^2
expressions.description: Assuming the object moves at velocity sigma through the background

expressions.name: Deceleration (supersonic, v_M >> sigma)
expressions.expression: dv = 4 * pi * G^2 * M * m_p * n / sigma^2 * log(Lambda); dv in cm/s^2
expressions.description: Assuming the object moves at velocity sigma >> velocity dispersion

expressions.name: Decay timescale (BH in galaxy core)
expressions.expression: t_fric = 19 / Lambda * (r_i / 5)^2 * sigma / 200 * (1e8 / M); t_fric in Gyr
expressions.latex: t_{\rm fric} \approx \frac{19 \, {\rm Gyr}}{\ln \Lambda} \left(\frac{r_i}{5\,{\rm kpc}}\right)^2 \frac{\sigma}{200\,{\rm km/s}} \left(\frac{10^8 M_\odot}{M}\right)
expressions.description: Timescale for a BH to decay to galaxy center in an isothermal sphere

body: Dynamical friction is the drag force experienced by a massive object moving through a background of lighter particles. It governs the orbital decay of supermassive black holes and massive star clusters in galaxies.

title: Eddington Luminosity

expressions.name: Eddington luminosity
expressions.expression: L_edd = 4 * pi * G * M * c * m_p / sigma_T; L_edd in erg/s
expressions.latex: L_{\rm Edd} = \frac{4\pi G M c m_p}{\sigma_T}

expressions.name: Eddington accretion rate
expressions.expression: Mdot_edd = 4 * pi * G * M * m_p / (0.1 * sigma_T * c); Mdot_edd in M_sun/yr

body:
The Eddington luminosity is the maximum luminosity a star can have
while maintaining hydrostatic equilibrium.

title: Escape Velocity

expressions.name: Escape velocity
expressions.expression: v_esc = sqrt(2 * G * M / R); v_esc in km/s

expressions.name: Circular velocity
expressions.expression: v_circ = sqrt(G * M / R); v_circ in km/s
expressions.description: Circular orbital velocity, 1/√2 times escape velocity

body:
The escape velocity is the minimum speed needed for an object to escape
the gravitational influence of a massive body.

title: Free-Fall Time

expressions.name: Free-fall time
expressions.expression: t_ff = sqrt(3 * pi / (32 * G * mu * m_p * n)); t_ff in Myr

body: The free-fall time is the characteristic timescale for a pressureless, self-gravitating cloud to collapse under its own gravity. It sets the fundamental timescale for star formation.

title: Jeans Mass and Jeans Length

expressions.name: Jeans length
expressions.expression:
c_s = sqrt(k_B * T / (mu * m_p))
 lambda_J = c_s * sqrt(pi / (G * mu * m_p * n)); lambda_J in pc

expressions.name: Jeans mass
expressions.expression:
c_s = sqrt(k_B * T / (mu * m_p))
 lambda_J = c_s * sqrt(pi / (G * mu * m_p * n))
 M_J = (4/3) * pi * (lambda_J / 2)^3 * mu * m_p * n; M_J in M_sun

expressions.name: Bonnor-Ebert mass
expressions.expression: M_BE = 1.182 * sqrt(k_B * T / (mu * m_p))^3 / (G^3 * mu * m_p * n)^(1/2); M_BE in M_sun
expressions.description: Maximum stable mass for a pressure-confined isothermal sphere

body: The Jeans length and Jeans mass define the scale above which a self-gravitating gas cloud becomes unstable to gravitational collapse. The Bonnor-Ebert mass is the equivalent for a pressure-confined isothermal sphere.

title: Kelvin-Helmholtz Timescale

expressions.name: KH timescale
expressions.expression: tau = G * M^2 / (R * L); tau in Myr

body: The Kelvin-Helmholtz (thermal) timescale is the time for a star to radiate its gravitational binding energy at its current luminosity. It governs the contraction of protostars and pre-main-sequence stars.

title: Kepler's Third Law

expressions.name: Orbital period
expressions.expression: P = sqrt(4 * pi^2 * a^3 / (G * M)); P in yr

expressions.name: Orbital separation from period
expressions.description: Given period P, compute semi-major axis

body: Kepler's Third Law relates orbital period to semi-major axis for two-body systems.

title: Schwarzschild Radius

expressions.name: Schwarzschild radius
expressions.expression: r_s = 2 * G * M / c^2; r_s in km

body: The Schwarzschild radius defines the event horizon of a non-rotating black hole.

title: Sedov-Taylor Blast Wave

expressions.name: Shock radius
expressions.expression: r_sh = (E * t^2 / (mu * m_p * n))^(1/5); r_sh in pc

body: The Sedov-Taylor phase describes the adiabatic expansion of a strong blast wave, such as a supernova remnant, before radiative losses become significant. The shock conserves energy during this self-similar phase.

title: Sound Speed

params.description: Mean particle weight (mu = 0.5 for ionized gas)
Adiabatic index (1 for isothermal, 5/3 for adiabatic)

expressions.name: Sound speed
expressions.expression: c_s = sqrt(gamma * k_B * T / (mu * m_p)); c_s in km/s

expressions.name: Isothermal sound speed (gamma=1)
expressions.expression: c_s = 0.29 * sqrt(T / 10 / mu); c_s in km/s

body: The sound speed sets the characteristic velocity of pressure waves in a gas. In astrophysics, the isothermal sound speed is most commonly used for cold molecular gas.

title: Strömgren Radius

params.description: Case B recombination coefficient (~2.6e-13 at 10^4 K)

expressions.name: Strömgren radius
expressions.expression: R_S = (3 * Q0 / (4 * pi * n^2 * alpha_B))^(1/3); R_S in pc

body: The Strömgren radius is the size of the ionized region (H II region) around a young, massive star, set by the balance between photoionization and radiative recombination. alpha_B(T) is the Case B recombination coefficient.

title: Toomre Q Parameter

expressions.name: Toomre Q (Keplerian)
expressions.expression: Q = Omega * c_s / (pi * G * Sigma)

expressions.name: Toomre Q (flat rotation)
expressions.expression: Q = sqrt(2) * Omega * c_s / (pi * G * Sigma)

expressions.name: Toomre Q (rigid rotation)
expressions.expression: Q = 2 * Omega * c_s / (pi * G * Sigma)

body: The Toomre Q parameter measures the stability of a rotating disk against gravitational collapse. The disk is unstable when Q < 1. The expression depends on the rotation curve: Keplerian, flat, or rigid.
